data.title: bioAffinity Technologies Stock Soars 120% On CyPath Lung Sales Growth
data.description:
bioAffinity Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:BIAF) shares are trading sharply higher Friday morning after the company reported full-year 2025 results highlighting strong growth in its flagship CyPath Lung diagnostic, even as total revenue declined following a strategic shift away from lower-margin lab services. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • bioAffinity Technologies stock is surging to new heights today. What’s behind BIAF gains?

CyPath Lung Revenue Surges 87% As Test Volume Jumps

The company said CyPath Lung revenue rose 87% year over year in 2025, while the number of tests performed jumped 99%. Orders from physician offices and clinics increased 67%, which bioAffinity attributed to stronger awareness, peer-to-peer physician marketing and positive real-world clinical experience with the test.

Net Loss Widens Amid Warrants, Sales Expansion Spending

Net loss widened to $14.9 million from $9.0 million a year earlier, driven in part by changes in the fair value of warrants, expanded sales activity and increased clinical development spending. Cash and cash equivalents improved to $6.5 million at year-end, up from $1.1 million, after the company raised about $16.9 million in 2025 financings.

Looking ahead, bioAffinity said it expects CyPath Lung unit sales to increase more than 100% in 2026 as it expands commercialization and clinical validation efforts.

bioAffinity Stock Momentum Pushes RSI Into Overbought Territory

The Relative Strength Index (RSI) for bioAffinity Technologies fluctuated mostly within the neutral 30–70 range over the past year, with occasional dips into oversold territory and several brief spikes above the overbought threshold.

On Friday, the RSI surged above 70, signaling strong momentum and suggesting the stock may be entering overbought conditions after the latest rally.

BIAF Shares Surge Friday Morning

BIAF Price Action: bioAffinity Technologies shares were up 124.30% at $2.40 at the time of publication on Friday. The stock is near its 52-week low of $0.69, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: UK Economy Stalls As Mideast Oil Shock Reignites BoE Rate‑Cut Risks
data.description:
The United Kingdom’s economy stalled in January as weakening services and industrial output coincided with oil prices surging past $100 a barrel this week amid the war in the Middle East, pushing up British energy and household bills.

The turmoil hit just as the UK was showing signs of recovery, with higher energy costs feeding inflation, eroding household spending, and squeezing firms. These risks could derail the Bank of England's (BoE) rate‑cut decisions.

GDP was flat in January, the Office for National Statistics (ONS) said Friday, reversing 0.1% growth in December and 0.2% in November. Economists had expected a 0.2% rise, according to Trading Economics.

"The war in the Middle East and the consequent oil price rise will raise inflation and reduce consumer spending," T. Rowe Price economist Tomasz Wieladek said. "The current oil price shock will most likely drive inflation higher and tip the economy into recession, raising unemployment and cutting output. Stagflation is near."

For further reading: Europe in Review: Natural Gas Prices Spike as Europe Splits Over US‑Israeli Strikes

Oil extended gains this week after interruptions to shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, a chokepoint for about 20% of global crude flows. The disruption is among the most severe in recent years for the oil market.

Industrial Production Declines

The fall in employment activity was the largest negative contribution from a single industry to both services output and real GDP growth, the ONS said. It contributed -0.08 percentage points to services output and -0.06 percentage points to real GDP growth.

Industrial production also weakened. It fell 0.1% month-on-month in January, ONS data showed, missing expectations of 0.2%. It improved from a 0.9% decline in December.

Weakness in mining, quarrying, and energy generation drove the fall, the ONS said. Output of crude petroleum and natural gas declined 4.6%.

"The surprise stagnation in January's GDP challenges our view that the economy would rebound early in the year, before the Middle East conflict," Bloomberg economists Ana Andrade, Matt Bunny, and Dan Hanson wrote. "Higher energy prices pose an additional threat to that outlook."

The pound traded around $1.33 on Friday, extending recent losses amid broad dollar strength

UK Wholesale Energy Prices Surge

Wholesale energy prices in the UK have already increased amid the escalating tensions between Iran, the US, and Israel.

The System Average Price of gas increased by 68% in the week to March 8 versus the prior week, ONS data showed. The system price of electricity jumped 74% over the same weekly period.

The latest figures underscore the UK's vulnerability to global energy shocks. British households and firms are already feeling the inflationary hit, a pressure that may delay the BoE's long‑anticipated easing cycle.

Economic Data, War Will Change BoE's Rate Thinking

The conflict in the Middle East has upended the European Central Bank's equation, with traders increasing their bets on further interest‑rate hikes. Investors now expect the BoE to keep interest rates higher for longer in response to the energy shock, according to KPMG UK chief economist Yael Selfin.

For further reading: Middle East Tensions Push Energy Prices Higher, Pressuring ECB to Reassess Its Rate Policy

That's raising the strain on firms already grappling with higher input costs. Around 25% of trading businesses reported that their turnover had decreased in February, ONS data showed. That is down 6 percentage points compared with January.

This complicates the BoE's stance, Wieladek said. He urged the bank to hold rates and reaffirm its 2% inflation target.

"The BOE should keep rates on hold and prepare the public for the prospect of further hikes," he said.

Political Fallout for Starmer

The economic strain is reshaping UK politics. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under pressure as Iran tensions and weak data hit his standing at home and abroad.

The YouGov voting intention poll on Wednesday for The Times and Sky News showed Reform UK leading on 23% of the vote, with Labour at 17%. The Conservatives hold a 19% vote share, up three points on last week, with the Greens likewise on 19%.

"There was no growth at all in the UK economy in January," Matt Goodwin, a British academic, writer, pollster, and campaigner, wrote on X. "And this is before the impact of Iran. Labour has killed our economy."

Starmer's relations with Washington have also cooled after he initially blocked U.S. access to British bases, later allowing them for "defensive" missions. The decision drew criticism from officials and politicians in Washington.

"I'm not a big fan of Prime Minister Starmer," Louisiana Senator John Kennedy said on March 10. "The United Kingdom was founded by geniuses, but at the moment it's being run by idiots. They deserve better than the government they are being given right now."

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga's reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: What's Going On With SOLAI Stock Today?
data.description:
SOLAI Limited (NYSE:SLAI) shares are down on Friday morning following a preliminary non-binding proposal from Chaince Digital Holdings Inc. to acquire all outstanding shares at a price of $3.069 per ADS.

• SOLAI stock is feeling bearish pressure. What’s behind SLAI decline?

The proposal represents a purchase price that is 110% of the company’s net asset value per ordinary share as of Sept. 30, 2025.

The final purchase price will be adjusted based on the most recent quarter-end net asset value, with a cap not to exceed $3.20 per ADS.

In addition, the board of directors has yet to make any decisions regarding the proposal, indicating that there is no guarantee of a definitive offer or agreement being executed. This uncertainty may contribute to the stock’s decline as investors weigh the implications of the potential acquisition.

Cash and Cryptocurrency Assets

As of Sept. 30, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of $3.8 million, compared with cash and equivalents of $1.8 million as of Dec. 31, 2024.

The broader market experienced gains on the previous trading day, with the S&P 500 rising by 0.56% and the Technology sector gaining 0.65%. SLAI’s decline came as the broader market moved higher, suggesting that company-specific factors may be influencing the stock’s performance.

Technical Analysis

Currently, the stock is trading 13.1% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 5% above its 50-day SMA, indicating some short-term strength.

However, shares have decreased by 45.60% over the past 12 months. The RSI is at 60.52, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is at -0.0053, with the signal line at -0.0271, indicating bullish momentum as the MACD is above the signal line. The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $1
  • Key Support: 63 cents

SOLAI is a technology-driven cryptocurrency infrastructure company. It is expanding from its foundation in crypto mining to build a blockchain-based ecosystem spanning AI, stablecoins and payment infrastructure, and Solana treasury and staking operations supporting use cases across institutional settlement, commerce, consumer payments and AI-native agent transactions.

The company is leveraging its blockchain and data infrastructure expertise, aiming to enhance on-chain efficiency and expand participation across Solana and other blockchain ecosystems. This strategic direction is particularly relevant in light of the recent acquisition proposal, as it reflects the company’s ongoing evolution and potential future value.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

The countdown is on: SOLAI is set to report earnings on March 24, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 5 cents (Up from Loss of 16 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $7.20 million (Down from $8.79 million)

SLAI Price Action

SLAI Stock Price Activity: Solai shares were down 5.06% at 94 cents at publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: Aree S via Shutterstock

data.title: New Data Shows A Surprising Rebound In Tech Hiring. Software Engineer Job Postings Are 'Rapidly Rising' And Are Up 11% Year Over Year
data.description:
For much of the past two years, the conversation around artificial intelligence has centered on one fear: that machines would soon start replacing white-collar workers at scale. But new labor market data suggests something very different may be happening.

Instead of disappearing, many tech jobs appear to be coming back. According to a new analysis from Citadel Securities, job postings for software engineers are “rapidly rising” and are now up about 11% year over year.

Tech Hiring Rebounds Despite AI Anxiety

The data, based on job postings tracked by job site Indeed, shows a clear rebound in demand for software engineers even as companies pour massive investments into artificial intelligence.

Don't Miss:

The broader job market has remained relatively steady as well. Citadel added that the unemployment rate recently printed at 4.28%, a level that still signals a resilient labor market.

At the same time, companies are spending aggressively on AI infrastructure. Citadel estimates that AI capital expenditures have reached about 2% of U.S. GDP, or roughly $650 billion. Commodities tied to the AI boom have surged too, with “AI-adjacent commodities” up about 65% since early 2023.

Despite the narrative that AI will eliminate large numbers of jobs, the firm argues the data doesn't support that view.

“In spite the current displacement narrative – job postings for software engineers are rising rapidly,” the report said.

The hiring trend comes as the U.S. prepares for a massive buildout of computing infrastructure. Almost 3,000 data centers are currently planned across the country, according to data analytics company Aterio, which is also boosting employment in construction and related industries.

AI Adoption Still Moving Slowly

Another reason mass job displacement hasn't appeared yet is that AI adoption is still developing gradually inside workplaces.

Data from the St. Louis Fed's Real-Time Population Survey released in November shows more people were experimenting with generative AI tools, but daily use for work remained relatively stable at just under 40%.

Citadel argues that the key question isn't simply whether people have tried AI, but how often they rely on it to perform their jobs.

“The more important question insofar as it relates to the AI displacement narrative is: how intensely is AI being used for work?” the report said.

So far, the numbers show little evidence of a sudden shift. The share of workers using generative AI every day for their jobs remains relatively low, suggesting companies are still figuring out how to integrate the technology.

Productivity Shock, Not Economic Collapse

Rather than triggering mass unemployment, the firm argues AI may end up functioning more like earlier technological revolutions.

Historically, technologies such as electricity, computers and the internal combustion engine boosted productivity, lowered costs and expanded economic output.

In many cases, they also created entirely new industries and business models.

That possibility is already visible in another piece of data highlighted in the report: a surge in new business formation.

Applications to start new businesses in the U.S. have risen sharply over the past decade, reaching levels far above those seen in the early 2000s. In January, there were 532,319 new business applications, an increase of 7.2% compared to December, per the U.S. Census Bureau.

According to Citadel, that trend could reflect the emergence of new economic opportunities created by digital tools and automation.

The firm also points out that fully replacing large numbers of workers with AI would require enormous computing resources, energy and infrastructure. If the cost of running those systems rises above the cost of human labor for certain tasks, companies have little incentive to automate them.

“It seems more likely that AI will be a complement rather than a substitute for labor in many areas,” the report said.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Acurx Pharmaceuticals Stock Today?
data.description:
Acurx Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ:ACXP) shares are down during early trading on Friday following the company’s recent financial update, which revealed a net loss for the fourth quarter.

The company reported a net loss of 73 cents per diluted share, for the fourth quarter of 2025, beating the Street view of $1.65 loss.

Acurx ended the quarter with cash totaling $7.6 million, an increase from $3.7 million a year earlier, and raised approximately $1.5 million through its Equity Line of Credit during the fourth quarter.

The company is focused on developing a new class of antibiotics, with its lead candidate, Ibezapolstat, preparing for international Phase 3 clinical trials to treat C. difficile infection.

About The Company

Acurx Pharmaceuticals Inc is a clinical stage biopharmaceutical company developing a new class of antibiotics for infections caused by bacteria listed as priority pathogens by the World Health Organization, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and Food and Drug Administration.

The company’s approach is to develop antibiotic candidates with a Gram-positive selective spectrum, focusing on addressing difficult-to-treat bacterial infections.

This focus is particularly relevant as antibiotic resistance continues to rise globally, making the development of new therapies crucial.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Acurx Pharmaceuticals, Inc. Common Stock is slated to provide its next financial update on May 12, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: -$1.24 (Up from -$2.20)

ACXP Price Action: Acurx Pharmaceuticals shares were down 19.16% at $4.24 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: jittawit21/Shutterstock
